name: GitRace Manager
vision: F1 Manager game where GitHub contributions become car performance. Follows real F1 calendar with qualifying during the week and races on weekends. Grids of 20 drivers with divisions (F3→F2→F1), sprint races, rivalries, achievements. Mobile-first UI, no 3D.

tasks.title: Remove Three.js dependencies and 3D components
tasks.description: Remove three, @react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei, @types/three from package.json. Delete src/components/race/race-canvas.tsx, src/hooks/use-camera.ts, public/models/f1-car.glb, and src/lib/race/track-geometry.ts + its test. Run npm install after.
acceptance: No three.js related packages in package.json

hints: Also remove any imports of deleted files in other components

tasks.title: Clean up old race page and references to 3D
tasks.description: Update src/app/race/page.tsx to remove RaceCanvas import — replace with a placeholder 'Coming soon' page. Remove old race/[raceId] page. Clean up src/components/race/hud.tsx if it depends on 3D state. Update any imports in layout.tsx or other pages that reference removed components.

hints: Keep the route structure, just replace content

features.name: F1: Database Schema — Grand Prix, Divisions, Bots

tasks.title: Create migration: grand_prix table with F1 2025 calendar
tasks.description: Create supabase/migrations/002_grand_prix.sql. Table grand_prix: id uuid PK, season_id FK, slug text unique, name text, country text, country_code text (ISO 3166), circuit_name text, round_number int, theme_colors jsonb (primary, secondary, accent), has_sprint boolean, quali_start timestamptz, quali_end timestamptz, sprint_date timestamptz nullable, race_date timestamptz, status enum('upcoming','qualifying','sprint','race_day','finished'). Seed with full 2025 F1 calendar (24 GPs). Include RLS (public read, service_role write). Index on (season_id, status) and (race_date).
acceptance: All 24 GPs of 2025 season seeded with correct dates
Theme colors defined for each GP

hints: 2025 calendar: Bahrain 3/14-16, Saudi 3/21-23, Australia 3/28-30, Japan 4/4-6, China (sprint) 4/18-20, Miami (sprint) 5/2-4, Emilia Romagna 5/16-18, Monaco 5/23-25, Spain 6/1, Canada 6/13-15, Austria (sprint) 6/27-29, UK 7/4-6, Belgium (sprint) 7/25-27, Netherlands 8/29-31, Italy 9/5-7, Azerbaijan 9/19-21, Singapore 10/3-5, USA (sprint) 10/17-19, Mexico 10/24-26, Brazil (sprint) 10/31-11/2, Las Vegas 11/20-22, Qatar (sprint) 11/28-30, Abu Dhabi 12/5-7
Theme colors: use flag-inspired colors per country

tasks.title: Create migration: divisions and grids tables
tasks.description: Create supabase/migrations/003_divisions_grids.sql. Table divisions: id uuid PK, name text ('F3','F2','F1'), level int (1=F3, 2=F2, 3=F1), season_id FK. Table grids: id uuid PK, gp_id FK references grand_prix, division_id FK, created_at. Table grid_entries: id uuid PK, grid_id FK, profile_id FK, is_bot boolean default false, bot_name text nullable, bot_stats jsonb nullable. Add division_id column to profiles table (nullable FK). RLS: public read, service_role write. Unique constraint on (grid_id, profile_id).
acceptance: Migration creates divisions, grids, grid_entries tables

hints: New users default to division F3 (level 1)

tasks.title: Create migration: qualifying_results and race_results tables
tasks.description: Create supabase/migrations/004_qualifying_race_results.sql. Table qualifying_results: id uuid PK, gp_id FK, grid_id FK, profile_id FK, q1_time float, q2_time float nullable, q3_time float nullable, final_position int, eliminated_in text nullable ('Q1','Q2'), created_at. Table race_results: id uuid PK, gp_id FK, grid_id FK, profile_id FK, grid_position int, final_position int, points_earned int default 0, fastest_lap boolean default false, dnf boolean default false, dnf_lap int nullable, dnf_reason text nullable, gap_to_leader float default 0, events_log jsonb default '[]', is_sprint boolean default false, created_at. Unique on (gp_id, profile_id, is_sprint). RLS: public read, service_role write.
acceptance: Both tables created with correct columns
Indexes on (gp_id, final_position) for both tables

hints: events_log stores array of {lap, type, description, involved_profiles}

tasks.title: Create migration: achievements system
tasks.description: Create supabase/migrations/005_achievements.sql. Table achievements: id uuid PK, slug text unique, name text, description text, icon text (emoji), criteria jsonb, category text ('race','qualifying','season','contribution'). Table profile_achievements: id uuid PK, profile_id FK, achievement_id FK, unlocked_at timestamptz default now(), gp_id FK nullable. Unique on (profile_id, achievement_id). Seed initial achievements: pole_position, race_winner, podium, comeback_king, consistency_is_key, rain_master, senna (3 consecutive poles), the_professor (50+ reviews), rookie_of_the_year, first_points, first_win, hat_trick (pole+win+fastest_lap), grand_chelem (pole+win+fastest_lap+led_every_lap). RLS: public read, service_role write for achievements; public read + own insert for profile_achievements.

hints: criteria format: {type: 'race_position', value: 1} or {type: 'consecutive_poles', count: 3}

tasks.title: Add car_stats JSONB column to profiles and update activity_snapshots
tasks.description: Create supabase/migrations/006_car_components.sql. Add car_stats jsonb column to profiles (default: {power_unit:0, aero:0, reliability:0, tire_mgmt:0, strategy:0}). Add rival_id uuid FK nullable to profiles (self-referencing). Update activity_snapshots to include component breakdown columns: power_unit_score float, aero_score float, reliability_score float, tire_mgmt_score float, strategy_score float (all default 0). These replace the old generic speed/consistency/impact scores for the new 5-component model.
acceptance: activity_snapshots has 5 new component score columns
Old speed_score/consistency_score/impact_score columns preserved for backwards compat

hints: Keep old score columns — they'll be deprecated but not removed yet

tasks.title: Create car component calculation module
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/car-components.ts. Define CarStats interface {power_unit, aero, reliability, tire_mgmt, strategy} (all 0-100). Implement calculateCarComponents(metrics: ActivityMetrics, days: DayContribution[]): CarStats. Mapping: power_unit = log2(commits * 3 + 1) * 10 capped at 100. aero = log2((prs_opened * 5 + prs_merged * 8) + 1) * 10 capped at 100. reliability = (activeDays / totalDays) * 100. tire_mgmt = log2(prs_reviewed * 4 + 1) * 10 capped at 100. strategy = log2((issues_opened * 2 + issues_closed * 3) + 1) * 10 capped at 100. Export helper calculateOverallRating(stats: CarStats): number (weighted average).
acceptance: All 5 components calculated correctly from GitHub metrics

hints: Reuse ActivityMetrics and DayContribution interfaces from metrics.ts

tasks.title: Write unit tests for car component calculations
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/car-components.test.ts. Test: zero activity = all zeros. Test: only commits → only power_unit increases. Test: only PRs → only aero increases. Test: daily activity → reliability 100. Test: only reviews → only tire_mgmt increases. Test: only issues → only strategy increases. Test: all maxed out caps at 100 each. Test: overall rating calculation. Test: realistic developer profile (mixed contributions).

hints: Follow same test style as metrics.test.ts

tasks.title: Update GitHub sync to calculate car components
tasks.description: Modify src/lib/github/sync.ts syncProfileActivity to also call calculateCarComponents and include the 5 component scores in the returned snapshot data. Update syncAllProfiles to also update profiles.car_stats with latest component values. Keep old speed/consistency/impact scores for now (backwards compat).
acceptance: Old scores still calculated and stored

hints: Import calculateCarComponents from car-components.ts

features.name: F3: Grand Prix Lifecycle & Calendar Engine

tasks.title: Create GP calendar data module
tasks.description: Create src/lib/f1/calendar.ts. Export F1_2025_CALENDAR array with all 24 GPs containing: slug, name, country, countryCode, circuit, round, hasSprint, dates (qualiStart, qualiEnd, sprintDate, raceDate as ISO strings), themeColors {primary, secondary, accent, bg}. Export helpers: getCurrentGP(), getNextGP(), getGPBySlug(slug), getGPStatus(gp) returning 'upcoming'|'qualifying'|'sprint'|'race_day'|'finished', isQualifyingActive(gp), isRaceDay(gp). This is a static data file — no DB calls.
acceptance: All 24 GPs defined with correct dates
Sprint weekends flagged (China, Miami, Austria, Belgium, USA, Brazil, Qatar)
getCurrentGP returns correct GP based on today's date
Theme colors defined for each GP

hints: Qualifying: Monday 00:00 UTC to Friday 23:59 UTC of GP week
Sprint: Saturday of sprint weekends
Race: Sunday, use approximate real GP times

tasks.title: Write tests for GP calendar helpers
tasks.description: Create src/lib/f1/calendar.test.ts. Test getCurrentGP with mocked dates. Test getGPStatus for each lifecycle stage. Test sprint weekend detection. Test getNextGP when between GPs. Test edge case: off-season (no active GP).

hints: Use vi.useFakeTimers() in vitest to mock dates

tasks.title: Create GP theme provider component
tasks.description: Create src/components/theme-provider.tsx. Server component that reads current GP from calendar, extracts theme colors, and applies them as CSS custom properties on a wrapper div (--gp-primary, --gp-secondary, --gp-accent, --gp-bg). Also passes GP metadata (name, country, countryCode, circuit) via React context. Create src/lib/f1/theme-context.ts with GPThemeContext and useGPTheme hook. Update src/app/layout.tsx to wrap children with ThemeProvider.
acceptance: ThemeProvider sets CSS custom properties based on current GP
useGPTheme hook returns current GP info

hints: Use CSS custom properties so Tailwind classes can reference them via arbitrary values

tasks.title: Create qualifying engine
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/qualifying.ts. Implement calculateLapTime(carStats: CarStats, noise: number): number — converts car stats to lap time (lower is better). Base time ~90s, each component reduces time. Implement runQualifying(drivers: QualifyingDriver[]): QualifyingResult[] — simulates Q1 (all 20, eliminate bottom 5), Q2 (15, eliminate bottom 5), Q3 (top 10 fight for pole). Each session: lapTime = calculateLapTime(stats) + small random variance. Export types QualifyingDriver {profileId, name, carStats, isBot} and QualifyingResult {profileId, q1Time, q2Time?, q3Time?, finalPosition, eliminatedIn?}.
acceptance: Lap times inversely correlated with car stats

hints: Use seeded RNG for deterministic results — seed = gpSlug + profileId

tasks.title: Write qualifying engine tests
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/qualifying.test.ts. Test: driver with best stats gets pole (most of the time). Test: Q1/Q2 elimination works correctly (5 eliminated each). Test: 20 drivers produce 20 results. Test: deterministic with same seed. Test: bots participate like real drivers. Test: all drivers have valid lap times (no NaN/Infinity).

tasks.title: Create qualifying update cron API route
tasks.description: Create src/app/api/cron/update-qualifying/route.ts. GET handler: verify CRON_SECRET. Check if current GP is in qualifying phase (Mon-Fri). For each grid in active GP: fetch latest activity_snapshots for all grid members, calculate car components, run qualifying simulation, upsert results to qualifying_results table. Return summary JSON.
acceptance: Handles bots (generates random stats for them)

hints: Follow same auth pattern as existing cron routes

tasks.title: Create race simulation engine with narrative events
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/simulation.ts. Implement simulateRace(drivers: RaceDriver[], config: RaceConfig): RaceSimulationResult. RaceConfig: {totalLaps: number, hasSprint: boolean, seed: string, weatherRoll: number}. Simulation loop per lap: calculate overtake probability (motor stat vs defender aero), pit stop events (strategy stat), DNF probability (inverse of reliability), safety car (random, 5% per lap), rain (random, based on weatherRoll). Generate narrative events array: {lap, type, description, involvedDrivers}. Event descriptions should be exciting F1-style text. Types: 'overtake', 'defense', 'pit_stop', 'dnf', 'safety_car', 'rain', 'fastest_lap', 'drs'. Deterministic given same seed.
acceptance: Simulation produces positions for all 20 drivers
Events generated for 50-70 laps (race) or 15-25 laps (sprint)
DNF possible for low reliability drivers

hints: Use simple seeded PRNG: seed-based LCG or xorshift
Narrative templates: 'Lap {n}: {driver} overtakes {other} with DRS on the main straight!'
Keep ~20-40 events per race, not one per lap

tasks.title: Write race simulation tests
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/simulation.test.ts. Test: 20 drivers in, 20 results out (some may DNF). Test: higher stats driver generally finishes better. Test: deterministic with same seed. Test: events array is non-empty and has correct structure. Test: sprint race has fewer laps. Test: safety car event reshuffles gaps. Test: DNF drivers get 0 points. Test: fastest lap awarded to one driver only.
acceptance: Statistical tests allow for RNG variance

hints: For statistical tests, run simulation 100 times and check majority trend

tasks.title: Update scoring module for sprint race points
tasks.description: Update src/lib/race/scoring.ts. Add SPRINT_POINTS = [8,7,6,5,4,3,2,1]. Add getSprintPointsForPosition(position). Update calculateRacePoints to accept isSprint parameter. No fastest lap bonus in sprints. Update StandingsEntry to include sprint results separately. Update buildSeasonStandings to handle both race and sprint entries.
acceptance: No fastest lap bonus in sprint

hints: Add new tests for sprint scoring

tasks.title: Create race simulation cron API route
tasks.description: Create src/app/api/cron/simulate-race/route.ts. GET handler with CRON_SECRET auth. Check if today is race day (or sprint day) for current GP. For each grid: fetch qualifying results for grid positions, fetch car stats for all drivers, run simulateRace, store results in race_results table, update profiles.total_points, check and award achievements. For sprint weekends: run sprint on Saturday, race on Sunday. Return summary.
acceptance: Stores full event log in race_results.events_log

hints: Follow same pattern as qualifying cron

tasks.title: Create matchmaking and bot generation module
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/matchmaking.ts. Implement assignToGrid(profileId, divisionId, gpId): assigns player to a grid in their division for the GP. If no grid has space (<20), create new one. Implement fillGridWithBots(gridId): fill remaining slots to 20 with bots. Bot names: use F1 legend names (Senna, Prost, Schumacher, etc. — 30+ names). Bot stats: random but clustered by division (F3=low, F2=mid, F1=high). Implement promoteRelegate(gpId): after race, top 3 of each grid move up a division, bottom 3 move down. F1 top stays, F3 bottom stays.
acceptance: New users assigned to F3 grid
Bot stats appropriate for division level

hints: Bot profile entries use is_bot=true in grid_entries, no real profile row needed

tasks.title: Write matchmaking and bot tests
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/matchmaking.test.ts. Test: new player gets F3 grid. Test: grid fills to exactly 20 with bots. Test: bots have appropriate stats per division. Test: promotion moves top 3 up. Test: relegation moves bottom 3 down. Test: F1 top and F3 bottom stay put. Test: multiple grids created when first is full.

hints: Mock Supabase client for unit tests

tasks.title: Create division promotion/relegation cron API route
tasks.description: Create src/app/api/cron/update-divisions/route.ts. GET handler with CRON_SECRET auth. Runs after race simulation. For each grid of finished GP: get final standings, promote top 3, relegate bottom 3, update profiles.division_id. Log movements for display on leaderboard.

hints: Can be triggered as part of simulate-race cron, or separately

tasks.title: Add grid assignment to auth callback
tasks.description: Update src/app/(auth)/callback/route.ts. After creating/updating profile: check if user has a grid for the current GP. If not, call assignToGrid to place them in an F3 grid. Ensure first sync runs and car_stats are initialized.
acceptance: New users get assigned to F3 grid on first login
Existing users without grid for current GP get assigned

hints: Handle case where there's no active GP (off-season)

tasks.title: Create rivalry detection module
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/rivalries.ts. Implement detectRivals(profileId, gridId): find 1-2 closest drivers in championship standings within the same grid. Consider: point difference < 15, position difference <= 3. Implement updateRivalries(gpId): after each race, recalculate rivalries for all drivers in all grids. Store rival_id on profiles table. Implement getRivalComparison(profileId, rivalId): returns side-by-side stats comparison {yourStats, rivalStats, yourPoints, rivalPoints, headToHead: {wins, losses}}.
acceptance: Rivals are closest in championship within same grid

hints: Rivalry can be with a bot too — adds narrative

features.name: F8: Home Page with GP Theme

tasks.title: Create home page hero with GP countdown and theme
tasks.description: Rewrite src/app/page.tsx. Server component that fetches: current/next GP from calendar, top 3 from global championship (from Supabase). Render: hero section with GP name, country flag emoji, circuit name, countdown timer to next event (qualifying or race). Apply GP theme colors via CSS custom properties. Show GP status badge ('Qualifying Live', 'Race Day', 'Next Race In X Days'). Below hero: top 3 drivers preview cards. CTA button 'Sign in with GitHub to race' for unauthenticated users, 'Go to Dashboard' for authenticated.
acceptance: Hero shows current/next GP with correct info
Countdown timer works (client component for timer only)

hints: Extract countdown to a small client component, keep rest as server component

tasks.title: Create countdown timer client component
tasks.description: Create src/components/countdown-timer.tsx. Client component that takes targetDate: string prop. Shows days:hours:minutes:seconds countdown. Updates every second. When target is past, shows 'LIVE' or 'Finished' based on context prop. Styled with GP theme colors. Responsive: large on desktop, compact on mobile.
acceptance: Shows 'LIVE' when past start but before end

hints: Use useEffect with setInterval, cleanup on unmount

features.name: F9: Dashboard — Driver Card & Car Components

tasks.title: Create driver card component
tasks.description: Create src/components/driver/driver-card.tsx. Visual card inspired by F1/FIFA cards. Props: profile (avatar, username, carNumber, division, carStats, totalPoints, championshipPosition). Layout: avatar at top, name + number, division badge (F3=bronze, F2=silver, F1=gold), 5 component bars (power_unit, aero, reliability, tire_mgmt, strategy) with labels and values, overall rating circle. Card border color based on division. Use Tailwind for all styling.

hints: Replace existing driver-card.tsx completely with new design

tasks.title: Create car components visualization
tasks.description: Create src/components/dashboard/car-stats.tsx. Shows 5 car components as styled progress bars or radar chart. Each component: icon/emoji, name, value (0-100), progress bar colored by GP theme. Include tooltip explaining how to improve each component (e.g., 'Improve Power Unit by pushing more commits'). Show delta from last GP (+5, -2 etc.). Overall car rating as a large number.

hints: Delta requires fetching previous GP's activity_snapshot

tasks.title: Rebuild dashboard page with new components
tasks.description: Rewrite src/app/dashboard/page.tsx and dashboard-client.tsx. Server component fetches: user profile with car_stats, current GP info, grid assignment, qualifying position (if qualifying active), rival comparison, recent achievements. Layout: driver card (left/top on mobile), car stats (center), next event card with status (qualifying live / race countdown), rival comparison card, recent achievements. Remove old sync button and color picker from v1. Add 'Sync Now' small button that hits /api/github/sync.
acceptance: Dashboard shows driver card with current stats
Rival comparison shown if rival exists

hints: Keep dashboard-client.tsx for interactive parts (sync button, etc.)

tasks.title: Create qualifying grid display component
tasks.description: Create src/components/qualifying/qualifying-grid.tsx. Shows all 20 drivers in qualifying order. For each: position, driver name (or bot name), avatar, lap time, gap to P1, eliminated badge (if out in Q1/Q2). Highlight current user's row. Visual separation between Q3 (top 10, gold bg), Q2 (11-15, silver bg), Q1 eliminated (16-20, red bg). Animate position changes on data refresh.

hints: Use CSS transitions for smooth position changes

tasks.title: Create qualifying page with live status
tasks.description: Create src/app/gp/[slug]/qualifying/page.tsx. Server component: fetch GP by slug from calendar, fetch qualifying_results for user's grid. Show: GP header (name, circuit, flag), qualifying status (Q1/Q2/Q3 based on time in qualifying period — first 33% Q1, next 33% Q2, last 33% Q3), qualifying grid component, countdown to qualifying end. If qualifying not started: show 'Qualifying starts [date]'. If finished: show final grid with 'Grid Locked' badge.
acceptance: Page renders for valid GP slug

hints: Q1/Q2/Q3 phases are simulated based on time elapsed in qualifying period

tasks.title: Create race event timeline component
tasks.description: Create src/components/race/event-timeline.tsx. Scrollable timeline of race events. Each event: lap number badge, event icon (overtake=arrows, pit=wrench, dnf=red flag, safety_car=yellow, rain=cloud, fastest_lap=purple), narrative text, involved drivers with mini avatars. Filter buttons: All, Overtakes, Pit Stops, Incidents. Highlight events involving current user or their rival in a different color. Auto-scroll to latest event option.
acceptance: Timeline renders events chronologically by lap

hints: Events come from race_results.events_log JSONB field

tasks.title: Create podium celebration component
tasks.description: Create src/components/race/podium.tsx. Visual podium for top 3 finishers. P1 center (tallest), P2 left, P3 right. Each: driver avatar (large), name, team/division badge, points earned, position change from grid (green up arrow, red down). Confetti CSS animation on page load. Styled with GP theme colors.

hints: CSS confetti: use @keyframes with random positioned colored squares

tasks.title: Create race results table component
tasks.description: Create src/components/race/results-table.tsx. Full classification table: position, driver (avatar+name), grid position, position change (+/-), laps completed, gap to leader, pit stops, points earned, fastest lap indicator (purple). DNF drivers at bottom with DNF badge and reason. Highlight current user row.

hints: Reuse similar table patterns from existing leaderboard components

tasks.title: Create race result page
tasks.description: Create src/app/gp/[slug]/race/page.tsx. Server component: fetch GP by slug, fetch race_results for user's grid, fetch qualifying_results for grid comparison. Layout: GP header with theme, podium (top 3), event timeline, full results table, share button. If race hasn't happened yet: show countdown. If sprint weekend: tabs for Sprint and Race results. Share button generates URL /gp/[slug]/result/[username].
acceptance: Page renders for valid GP slug
Podium, timeline, results table all rendered

hints: Sprint results use is_sprint=true in race_results query

tasks.title: Create GP calendar page
tasks.description: Create src/app/calendar/page.tsx. Server component showing all 24 GPs in a vertical timeline. Each GP card: round number, GP name, country flag, circuit name, date range, status badge (finished/qualifying/upcoming), user's result if finished (position, points). Current/active GP highlighted with glow effect. Sprint weekends marked with sprint badge. Clicking a GP links to /gp/[slug]/qualifying or /gp/[slug]/race depending on status.
acceptance: User results shown for finished GPs

hints: Fetch user's race_results for all finished GPs in one query

tasks.title: Rebuild leaderboard page with divisions
tasks.description: Rewrite src/app/leaderboard/page.tsx. Server component with tabs: user's division grid standings, F1 Division (top), Global Championship. Each tab shows standings table: position, driver card mini (avatar+name+number), division badge, total points, wins, podiums, position trend mini-chart (last 5 GPs). Promotion zone (top 3) in green, relegation zone (bottom 3) in red. Highlight current user.

hints: Fetch race_results grouped by profile for stats calculation

tasks.title: Rebuild driver profile page
tasks.description: Rewrite src/app/driver/[username]/page.tsx. Server component. Fetch profile by github_username. Show: full driver card, season stats (races, wins, podiums, points, best finish, best qualifying), car components with history chart, race history table (all GPs: position, grid, points per GP), achievements/badges grid. If viewing own profile: show edit car color button. 404 if username not found.
acceptance: Race history shows all GP results

hints: Reuse driver-card component with full size variant

tasks.title: Create achievement checker module
tasks.description: Create src/lib/race/achievements.ts. Implement checkAchievements(profileId, gpId, raceResult, qualifyingResult): Achievement[]. Check each achievement criteria against results: pole_position (P1 in qualifying), race_winner (P1 in race), podium (top 3), comeback_king (gained 5+ positions), consistency_is_key (7/7 active days), rain_master (won race with rain event), senna (3 consecutive poles — query history), the_professor (50+ reviews in season — query activity_snapshots), first_points, first_win, hat_trick (pole+win+fastest), grand_chelem (pole+win+fastest+led_every_lap). Implement awardAchievements(profileId, achievements, gpId): insert into profile_achievements, skip if already earned.
acceptance: Historical checks work (consecutive poles, season totals)

hints: Some achievements need historical data — query past GP results

tasks.title: Create achievements display page
tasks.description: Create src/app/achievements/page.tsx. Server component showing all achievements in a grid. Each: icon, name, description, locked/unlocked state, unlock date and GP if unlocked. Categories: Race, Qualifying, Season, Contribution. Progress indicators for cumulative achievements (e.g., 'The Professor: 32/50 reviews'). Filter by category.

hints: Fetch all achievements + user's profile_achievements in one query

tasks.title: Create OG image route for race results
tasks.description: Create src/app/gp/[slug]/result/[username]/og/route.tsx. Use @vercel/og ImageResponse. Generate 1200x630 image showing: GP name + circuit, driver name + avatar, final position (large), grid → finish (e.g., P5 → P2), points earned, division badge. Styled with GP theme colors. This serves as the og:image for share URLs.
acceptance: OG image generates for valid GP + username

hints: Use Vercel OG with JSX, fetch data from Supabase

tasks.title: Create shareable result page
tasks.description: Create src/app/gp/[slug]/result/[username]/page.tsx. Public page showing one driver's result for a GP. Metadata: og:title, og:description, og:image pointing to the OG route. Content: mini podium if top 3, result card, position change, points, key events involving this driver. Twitter share button with pre-filled text: 'I finished P{x} at the {gpName}! 🏎️ {url}'. Link to full race results.
acceptance: Page renders correct result for driver+GP combo
Twitter share button with pre-filled text

hints: Use Next.js metadata export for OG tags

tasks.title: Update vercel.json cron configuration
tasks.description: Update vercel.json to add new cron routes: /api/cron/sync-activity (0 */6 * * * — every 6h, keep existing), /api/cron/update-qualifying (0 */6 * * 1-5 — every 6h Mon-Fri), /api/cron/simulate-race (0 14 * * 0 — Sundays at 14:00 UTC as default, actual trigger should check GP calendar), /api/cron/update-divisions (0 18 * * 0 — Sundays at 18:00 after race). Remove old /api/cron/calculate-race route. Update old sync-activity route to also trigger qualifying update if in qualifying phase.
acceptance: Old calculate-race route removed or replaced

hints: Race simulation ideally triggers at the actual GP race time, but a fixed Sunday schedule works for MVP

tasks.title: Global styles and GP theme CSS integration
tasks.description: Update src/app/globals.css. Define CSS custom properties for GP theming: --gp-primary, --gp-secondary, --gp-accent, --gp-bg with fallback defaults (F1 red/black). Add utility classes for common patterns: .card (rounded, shadow, border), .badge-f1/.badge-f2/.badge-f3 (division badges), .position-up/.position-down (green/red arrows), .stat-bar (progress bar base). Ensure dark mode support. Mobile-first breakpoints.

hints: Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first config — define custom utilities via @layer

tasks.title: Navigation bar with GP branding
tasks.description: Create src/components/nav.tsx. Responsive nav bar: logo/brand ('GitRace'), GP status indicator (current GP name + mini countdown), nav links (Dashboard, Calendar, Leaderboard, Achievements), user avatar dropdown (profile, settings, logout). Mobile: hamburger menu. Nav bar background uses GP accent color subtly.

hints: Add Nav to layout.tsx, conditionally show based on auth state
